LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JAPANESE WEEKEND INC. AND BARBIE WHITE'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, AS TO THE SOLE CAUSE OF ACTION IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF ZELELA MENKER'S SOLE CAUSE OF ACTION IS TIME-BARRED, ALBEIT ONLY BY A DAY. MS. MENKER DOES NOT CITE ANY STATUTE, TOLLING DOCTRINE OR CASE LAW SUPPORTING ANY OF HER CONTENTIONS THAT HER KIDNAPPING TOLLED THE LIMITATIONS PERIOD, FILING HER COMPLAINT A DAY AFTER THE LIMITATIONS PERIOD DOES NOT RENDER HER CLAIM TIME-BARRED OR LACK OF PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANTS SAVES HER CLAIM. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER THAT TRACKS THE TENTATIVE RULING VERBATIM AND SHOW COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: KELLEY DE YOUNG, CSR #6780. (302/HK).

Off Calendar Dec-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Cohen's Motion to Be Relieved As Council of Record for Plaintiff Zelela Menker is granted. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-02-2016 Continued To Dec-28-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-02-2016 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JAPANESE WEEKEND INC. and BARBIE WHITE'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the sole cause of action in the second amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff Zelela Menker's sole cause of action is time-barred, albeit only by a day. Ms. Menker does not cite any statute, tolling doctrine or case law supporting any of her contentions that her kidnapping tolled the limitations period, filing her complaint a day after the limitations period does not render her claim time-barred or lack of prejudice to defendants saves her claim. The prevailing party is to prepare a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and show compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Kelley De Young, CSR #6780. (302/HK)

MINI MINUTES:

MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF ZELELA MENKER'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is DENIED in the court's discretion. Granting the motion disserves judicial economy because the issues raised by the demurrer will still need to be resolved and can be the subject of a motion for judgment on the pleadings. Any opposition papers are due no later than October 12, 2016 and any reply papers are due no later than October 20, 2016. Counsel for defendants are reminded of their obligation under the local rules to deliver courtesy copies of all filed papers and the pleading that is at issue to department 302. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-15-2016 Continued To Sep-26-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-15-2016 Continued To Oct-05-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-31-2016 Continued To Aug-03-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jared Cohen of Law Offices of Jared M. Cohen telephone number 415-326-4044 appeared by telephone for Plaintiff Zelela Menker.
Norine Busser of The Law Offices of Melissa M. Ballard telephone number 714-371-2310 for Defendants Barbie White and Japanese Weekend, Inc.

Mr. Cohen requested clarification of the Court's ruling. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JAPANESE WEEKEND INC. and BARBIE WHITE'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained, with twenty days leave to amend. As currently pled, the action is barred by the statute of limitations. (CCP section 335.1.) If she can do so in good faith, Plaintiff is provided with an opportunity to amend the complaint to allege facts which demonstrate that it was impossible or virtually impossible for her to file the complaint within the two year statute of limitations imposed by CCP section 335.1. (See Lewis v. Superior Court (1985) 175 Cal.App.3d 366.) Plaintiff is also provided leave to amend the complaint if she can do so in good faith, to allege facts which show that Ms. White left California at some point during the statute of limitations period and that the statute of limitations was tolled for the period of time that Ms. White was not in California. (CCP section 351.) Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/SB)

Notice Of Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jared Cohen of Law Offices of Jared M. Cohen telephone number 415-326-4044 appeared by telephone for Plaintiff Zelela Menker.
Norine Busser of The Law Offices of Melissa M. Ballard telephone number 714-371-2310 for Defendants Barbie White and Japanese Weekend, Inc.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JAPANESE WEEKEND INC. and BARBIE WHITE'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint as to punitive damages is granted with twenty days leave to amend. To properly allege punitive damages in a motor vehicle accident action, a plaintiff needs to "establish that the defendant was aware of the probable dangerous consequences of his conduct, and that he wilfully and deliberately failed to avoid those consequences." (Taylor v. Superior Court (1979) 24 Cal.3d 890, 896.) The allegation that a driver drove while intoxicated, by itself, is not always enough to support a claim for punitive damages. (See Taylor v. Superior Court (1979) 24 Cal.3d 890, 892 ; Dawes v. Superior Court (1980) 111 Cal.App.3d 82, 89.) Here, Plaintiff is provided leave to amend the complaint to allege facts, if she can do so in good faith, regarding the level of Ms. White's intoxication and the circumstances of her driving. The Court also rules that the motion to strike paragraph 4 on page 5 of the addendum is denied, that language can be plead. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/SB)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

DEFENDANT JAPANESE WEEKEND INC and BARBIE WHITE'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT is off calendar. A first amended complaint was filed March 18, 2016 which rendered the demurrer to the complaint moot. "Because there is but one complaint in a civil action, the filing of an amended complaint moots a motion directed to a prior complaint." (State Compensation Insurance Fund v. Superior Court (2010) 184 Cal. App. 4th 1124, 1131 (internal citation omitted)).

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
